desc: Trebizat is really a river-valley base rather than a town break: swimming spots on the Trebižat, Kravica close by, wine country and Ottoman stone villages within easy reach, and enough shade-and-water appeal to balance the summer heat of lower Herzegovina. It makes most sense for travellers with a car who want to stay near the landscape instead of inside Mostar or on the Croatian coast.

todo.name: Kravica waterfalls
todo.desc: Visit early or outside peak summer hours for the best balance of water, light and crowding.

todo.name: Trebižat River
todo.desc: Use local advice for safe swimming spots and water levels along the river.

todo.name: Pocitelj side trip
todo.desc: Pair the river valley with the Ottoman stone village and Neretva viewpoints.

todo.name: Herzegovina wine tasting
todo.desc: Book a cellar visit around Capljina or Ljubuski to connect the trip with local Zilavka and Blatina wines.

todo.name: Kocusa waterfall side stop
todo.desc: Add Koćuša if you want a second waterfall stop with a more local, less headline feel than Kravica.

todo.name: Ceveljusa river mills
todo.desc: Look for older mill and riverside sections to understand the Trebižat as a lived-in working landscape, not just a swim hole.

wifi.note: Usually adequate in apartments and guesthouses near the main road, but riverside outings, cellar visits and waterfall areas are less dependable.

prac.fltNote: Trebizat is reached by road. Mostar is the nearest airport but its airline list is still thin, while Dubrovnik and Split give you broader route choice and both currently have airport-level London Gatwick service via easyJet. In practice the right gateway depends on whether you value the shortest inland transfer or the simplest UK nonstop.
prac.lang: Bosnian/Croatian/Serbian; English varies outside major tourist areas
prac.visa: British citizens can travel to Bosnia and Herzegovina without a visa for up to 90 days within a 6-month period, and GOV.UK says foreign nationals must register with the police within 72 hours of arrival if their accommodation does not handle this for them.
alerts: Kravica is crowded in peak summer afternoons.
River swimming depends on water level and local safety advice.
Border routes toward Croatia can slow in holiday traffic.
Travertine edges, wet rocks and improvised swimming entries can be slippery.

sweet: May, June and September are usually the best balance for river swimming, waterfall flow and manageable crowd levels before or after the hottest peak weeks.
